file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for HP ID (HPID) authentication operations including sign-in, sign-up, account verification, and country selection dismissal. Contains static methods for complete authentication flows used in test setup and user onboarding scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for HP ID authentication operations. Contains methods for dismissing country selector popups, performing sign-in with credentials, completing full sign-in flows, creating new user accounts with sign-up, verifying account codes via email, and completing full sign-up flows. All methods are static and work with Playwright page objects to automate HPID authentication processes.
classes.how_to_instantiate: HPIDHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) directly.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to dismiss the country selector popup that may appear on the HPID page. Creates an HPIDPage instance with the provided page object. Uses a try-except block to click the first country selector button using hpid_page.country_selector_button.first.click(). If the button is not found or any exception occurs during the click, the exception is silently caught and passed, allowing the flow to continue without failing. This is a defensive method used to handle optional UI elements that may or may not appear.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HPID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the core HPID sign-in flow by entering credentials and submitting the login form. Creates an HPIDPage instance. First fills the username field with the provided email using hpid_page.username.fill(email). Then clicks the username form submit button using hpid_page.user_name_form_submit.click() to proceed to the password entry step. Next fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Finally clicks the sign-in button using hpid_page.sign_in.click() to complete the authentication. This is a two-step login process where username and password are entered on separate screens.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HPID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HPID sign-in flow including handling optional UI elements. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup that may appear. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Then waits for the sign-in option button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.sign_in_option(timeout=20000) and clicks it to navigate to the sign-in page. Finally calls HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) to perform the actual credential entry and authentication. This is the recommended method for complete sign-in flows as it handles all pre-conditions and optional elements.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new user account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new user account

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new HPID user account by filling out the sign-up form with user details. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Fills the first name field with the hardcoded value 'InstantInk' using hpid_page.firstName.fill('InstantInk'). Fills the last name field with the hardcoded value 'User' using hpid_page.lastName.fill('User'). Fills the email field with the provided email address using hpid_page.email.fill(email). Fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Clicks the market checkbox using hpid_page.market.click() to accept terms. Finally clicks the create account button using hpid_page.create_account_button.click() to submit the registration form. After this method, account verification is typically required.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address where the verification code was sent

functions.what_it_does: Verifies a newly created HPID account by retrieving and entering the verification code sent via email. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the submit code button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.submit_code(timeout=20000). Implements a retry mechanism with 3 attempts and 10-second wait intervals. In each retry loop, waits for the specified time using page.wait_for_timeout(wait_time * 1000), then calls common.fetch_verification_code(email) to retrieve the verification code from the email system. If a verification code is successfully retrieved, fills the code input field using hpid_page.code.fill(verification_code), clicks the submit code button using hpid_page.submit_code.click(), and logs a success message with the user email and environment details using framework_logger.info(). If the code retrieval fails, logs a warning with the attempt number and exception details. If all retries fail, logs an error message and returns without completing verification. Finally waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load').

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for the new tenant user account

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HPID sign-up flow including account creation and verification. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the create account button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.create_account_button_data(timeout=20000) and clicks it to navigate to the sign-up page. Calls HPIDHelper.sign_up(page, tenant_email, common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD) to fill out and submit the registration form using the provided email and a default password from the common module. Finally calls HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code(page, tenant_email) to retrieve the verification code from email and complete the account verification process. This is the recommended method for complete user onboarding flows.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper class follows a static utility pattern with no instance state. All methods are static and operate on page objects passed as parameters. The class provides both granular methods (sign_in, sign_up) and composite methods (complete_sign_in, complete_sign_up) that combine multiple steps. The confirm_account_code method implements a robust retry mechanism for email verification code retrieval, which is critical for test reliability. The dismiss_select_country method uses defensive programming with try-except to handle optional UI elements gracefully. The sign_up method uses hardcoded values for firstName and lastName which may need to be parameterized for more flexible test scenarios. The complete_sign_up method uses common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD which suggests a shared test data configuration.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for landing page operations including page access, navigation, section validation, hyperlink verification, cookie settings, FAQ validation, country selection, and emergency banner validation. Supports both desktop (OOBE) and mobile (MOBE) viewport testing with extensive validation methods for all landing page components.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for testing and validating all aspects of the landing page including navigation, section visibility, hyperlink functionality, cookie preferences, FAQ interactions, country selection, and emergency banner validation. Supports both desktop (OOBE - Out of Box Experience) and mobile (MOBE - Mobile Out of Box Experience) viewport testing with separate methods for each platform. Contains methods for complete landing page flow validation, individual section verification, footer link validation, and dynamic content validation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: LandingPageHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call LandingPageHelper.access(page) directly.

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Instant Ink landing page URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() to navigate to the URL stored in common._instantink_url with a 120-second timeout. Then calls page.wait_for_load_state('load') to ensure the page has completed loading before proceeding. This is a basic navigation method that does not handle any popups or banners.

Navigates to the landing page and handles initial popups and banners. First calls LandingPageHelper.open(page) to navigate to the landing page URL. Creates a PrivacyBannerPage instance and calls privacy_banner_page.accept_privacy_banner() to accept the privacy consent banner if it appears. Creates a LandingPage instance and attempts to click the close banner button using landing_page.close_banner.click() within a try-except block. If the close banner is not found or cannot be clicked, the exception is caught and printed but does not fail the test. This is the recommended method for accessing the landing page as it handles common initial UI elements.

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Expected number of free months to be displayed in the RaF banner. Defaults to 1.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RaF) banner is visible on the landing page and contains the expected text about free months. Creates a LandingPage instance. Uses page.locator() with the raf_banner selector from landing_page.elements to locate the banner element. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible with a 30-second timeout. Retrieves the banner text content using raf_banner.text_content(). Constructs the expected text string as 'You have {expected_months} free month of HP Instant Ink'. Asserts that the expected text is present in the actual banner text using a Python assert statement with a descriptive error message. Logs a success message with the expected text using framework_logger.info(). Returns True if all validations pass.

Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RaF) footnote is visible on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance. Uses page.locator() with the raf_footnote selector from landing_page.elements to locate the footnote element. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the footnote is visible with a 30-second timeout. This is a simple visibility check without text content validation.

parameters.name: section_selector
parameters.description: CSS selector string for the section element to check

functions.what_it_does: Verifies whether a specific page section is currently visible within the browser viewport. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript code in the browser context. The JavaScript code takes the section_selector as a parameter, uses document.querySelector() to find the element, returns false if the element is not found, otherwise gets the element's bounding rectangle using getBoundingClientRect(), and checks if the element's top is at or below 0 and the bottom is at or below the window's inner height or document's client height. Returns a boolean value - true if the section is fully visible in the viewport, false otherwise. This method is useful for validating that anchor link navigation has scrolled the page to show the target section.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing custom actions at each validation step. Called with step name, page object, and screenshot_only flag.

functions.what_it_does: Performs comprehensive validation of all landing page sections and navigation functionality for desktop (OOBE) viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps all operations in a try-except block. Navigates directly to the stage landing page URL using page.goto(). Attempts to accept the privacy banner by waiting for and clicking the OneTrust accept button with a 10-second timeout, catching and logging if not found. Tests the Sign Up Now button by waiting for it to be visible with a 200-second timeout, clicking it, accepting privacy banner again if it appears, waiting for the HPID page to be visible, and calling the optional callback for screenshot. Logs success message. Returns to the landing page and tests each navigation section (How It Works, Instant Paper Plan, Plans, FAQs) by clicking the corresponding tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the section is visible, checking if the section is in viewport using verify_section_is_in_viewport(), logging success or error messages, and calling the optional callback for screenshots. Each section follows the same pattern of click, wait, verify visibility, verify viewport position, and log results. If any exception occurs during the entire flow, logs an error message with the exception details.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing custom actions at each validation step

functions.what_it_does: Performs comprehensive validation of all landing page sections and navigation functionality for mobile (MOBE) viewport. Similar to consumer_landing_page_oobe but adapted for mobile navigation patterns. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps all operations in a try-except block. Navigates to the stage landing page URL. Accepts privacy banner if present. Tests the mobile Sign Up flow by clicking the mobile menu button, waiting for the mobile sign up button to be visible, clicking it, accepting privacy banner again if needed, waiting for HPID page, and calling callback. Returns to landing page and opens mobile menu. Tests each navigation section (How It Works, Instant Paper Plan, Plans, FAQs) by opening the mobile menu before each section, clicking the corresponding mobile tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the section is visible, checking viewport position, logging results, and calling callback. The key difference from OOBE is that the mobile menu must be opened before each navigation action because mobile menus typically close after selection. Uses mobile-specific locators like mobe_menu_button, mobe_sign_up_button, mobe_how_it_works_tab, etc. If any exception occurs, logs an error message with exception details.

Validates that the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page for desktop viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps operations in a try-except block. Waits for the never_worry_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed using scroll_into_view_if_needed() with a 50-second timeout. Logs a success message indicating the section is visible. Calls the optional callback function with step name 'never_worry', page object, and screenshot_only=True flag. If any exception occurs, logs an error message with exception details.

Validates that the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page for mobile viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps operations in a try-except block. Waits for the mobe_never_work_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed using scroll_into_view_if_needed() with a 50-second timeout. Logs a success message indicating the section is visible. Calls the optional callback function with step name 'never_worry', page object, and screenshot_only=True flag. If any exception occurs, logs an error message with exception details. This is the mobile-specific version using the mobe_never_work_section locator.

Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page for desktop viewport by clicking each link, verifying the destination URL, taking optional screenshots, and closing the new tabs. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps all operations in a try-except block. For each footer link (HP.com, Privacy Statement, HP Connected Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Your Privacy Choices, Instant Ink Terms of Service), the method follows this pattern: waits for the link to be visible with a 50-second timeout, scrolls it into view if needed (for the first link), uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens when the link is clicked, clicks the link, waits for the new page to load with a 60-second timeout, adds a 5-second sleep for full page load on some links, uses Playwright's expect() with regex to verify the new page URL matches the expected pattern, logs a success message, calls the optional callback with a step name and the new page for screenshots, and closes the new tab. The validated links and their expected URL patterns are: HP.com (https://www.hp.com/us-en/home.html), Privacy Statement (https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/privacy.html), HP Connected Terms of Use (https://www.hpsmart.com/us/en/tou), Cookie Settings (https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/use-of-cookies.html), Your Privacy Choices (https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/your-privacy-choices.html), and Instant Ink Terms of Service (https://instantink-stage1.hpconnectedstage.com/us/en/terms). If any exception occurs during the validation, logs an error message with exception details.

Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page for mobile viewport. Identical to hyperlinks_validation() but sets the viewport size to mobile dimensions (400x740) for each new tab that opens. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps all operations in a try-except block. For each footer link, follows the same pattern as the desktop version: waits for link visibility, scrolls into view if needed, captures the new tab with expect_page(), clicks the link, sets the new page viewport to mobile size using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}), waits for page load, verifies the URL with regex, logs success, calls callback, and closes the tab. Validates the same six footer links with the same URL patterns as the desktop version. The key difference is the viewport size setting to ensure mobile-specific rendering is captured in screenshots. If any exception occurs, logs an error message with exception details.

Validates the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for desktop viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps operations in a try-except block. Waits for the footer cookies link to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new tab that opens when clicking the cookie settings link. Clicks the footer_cookies link. Waits for the new page to load with a 60-second timeout. Uses Playwright's expect() with regex to verify the new page URL is the HP cookie policy page (https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/use-of-cookies.html). Logs success message. Scrolls the cookies preference button into view if needed with a 50-second timeout. Waits for the cookies preference button to be visible and clicks it. Logs success message. Waits 5 seconds for the cookie preference modal to appear. Retrieves the text content of the cookies preference title element. Checks if the title text equals 'Privacy Preference Center' and logs success or error accordingly. Waits for the cookies preference close button to be visible. Calls the optional callback with step name 'cookie_preferences' and the new page for screenshot. Clicks the close button to dismiss the modal. Logs success message. Closes the new tab. If any exception occurs, logs an error message with exception details.

Validates the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for mobile viewport. Identical to cookies_settings_oobe() but sets the viewport size to mobile dimensions (400x740) for the new tab. Creates a LandingPage instance and wraps operations in a try-except block. Waits for the footer cookies link to be visible. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new tab. Clicks the footer_cookies link. Sets the new page viewport to mobile size using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}). Waits for page load and verifies the URL. Logs success. Scrolls the cookies preference button into view, waits for visibility, and clicks it. Logs success. Waits 5 seconds for modal. Retrieves and validates the title text equals 'Privacy Preference Center'. Waits for close button visibility. Calls callback for screenshot. Clicks close button and logs success. Closes the new tab. The method ends with an orphaned expect statement 'expect(raf_footnote).to_be_visible(timeout=30000)' which appears to be a copy-paste error and references an undefined variable. If any exception occurs, logs an error message with exception details.

Validates all links within the FAQ section of the landing page by expanding each FAQ category, clicking each question, and verifying all hyperlinks within the answers. Creates a LandingPage instance and stores the original page URL. Scrolls the FAQs section into view and verifies it is visible. Defines a list of FAQ sections with their names and IDs: General, Monthly plans, Yearly plans, and Pay As You Print plans. For each FAQ section, logs the section name, navigates back to the original URL if the page has changed, waits for network idle, locates the section tab using data-testid and aria-controls attributes, clicks the tab, waits 500ms, checks if a 'Show more' button exists and clicks it if present, locates all question buttons within the section using aria-controls attribute, iterates through each question by index, clicks the question, waits 300ms, gets the aria-controls ID, locates the answer element, verifies the answer is visible, locates all links within the answer, iterates through each link by index, gets the link href attribute, skips mailto and tel links, clicks the link, waits 2 seconds, gets all pages in the context, brings the last page to front, waits for network idle with 35-second timeout, attempts to verify the HP branding image is visible or the URL contains '/terms', logs success, closes the new page, navigates back to original URL if needed, waits for DOM content loaded, re-expands the section and question to continue with remaining links, catches and logs any link failures, collapses the question after processing all links, catches and logs any section failures, and finally navigates back to the original URL if it has changed. This is a comprehensive FAQ validation that handles dynamic content, multiple tabs, and navigation state management.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to select from the country selector. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific country from the country selector on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance. Clicks the flag icon to open the country selector using landing_page.flag_icon.click(). Loads the locale data mapping using load_locale_data() which returns a dictionary of country configurations. Retrieves the languages array for the specified country from the locale map. Retrieves the country code and converts it to lowercase. Selects the first language from the languages array. Constructs the locale code string in the format 'language-countrycode' (e.g., 'en-us'). Uses page.locator() with a data-testid attribute selector constructed from the locale code to find the country option. Clicks the country option to select it. This method is used to change the landing page locale for testing different country/language combinations.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to validate the emergency banner for. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the emergency banner on the landing page displays the correct color and icon for a specific country. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the emergency banner element and gets its parent element using locator('..'). Calls common.get_font_details() to retrieve CSS properties including background color. Extracts the detected background color from the returned data. Logs the detected color. Defines a country_configs dictionary mapping country names to their expected banner configurations including name, expected RGB color, and expected SVG path for the icon. Retrieves the configuration for the specified country. Logs the country and banner name being tested. Asserts that the detected color matches the expected color with a descriptive error message. Logs color validation success. If the country configuration includes an expected_icon_path (for countries like Finland, Portugal, Australia, Norway, Poland, Estonia), locates the SVG path element within the emergency banner, gets its 'd' attribute value, and asserts it matches the expected path with a descriptive error message, then logs SVG path validation success. If the country configuration has no expected_icon_path (for Bulgaria), locates the orange banner icon element by class name, asserts that at least one instance exists, and logs that the orange banner icon was found. Finally logs overall validation success for the banner type. The method supports validation for seven countries: Finland (Red Minus Banner), Bulgaria (Orange Alert Banner), Portugal (Green Done Banner), Australia (Blue Done Banner), Norway (White Done Banner), Poland (Grey Done Banner), and Estonia (Black Done Banner).

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is an extremely comprehensive helper class with 14 static methods covering all aspects of landing page testing. The class provides parallel implementations for desktop (OOBE) and mobile (MOBE) viewports, with methods suffixed accordingly. The consumer_landing_page_oobe and consumer_landing_page_mobe methods are particularly complex, performing full end-to-end validation of all landing page sections with navigation, visibility checks, viewport verification, and optional screenshot callbacks. The hyperlinks_validation methods thoroughly test all footer links by opening new tabs, verifying URLs, and closing tabs. The validate_faq_links method is highly sophisticated, handling dynamic FAQ expansion, nested link validation, and state management across multiple page navigations. The validate_new_emergency_banner method demonstrates advanced CSS property validation and country-specific configuration handling. The class uses extensive error handling with try-except blocks and detailed logging. The callback parameter pattern allows flexible integration with screenshot or reporting systems. The cookies_settings_mobe method has a bug at the end with an orphaned expect statement referencing an undefined variable. The class depends heavily on the LandingPage page object and various other page objects for element locators. All methods are static, making the class a pure utility module without instance state.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for OSS (Online Services Simulator) emulator operations including accessing the emulator, signing in, configuring printer details, selecting app types, starting flows, and handling various OOBE and POOBE (Post Out of Box Experience) scenarios. Used for testing printer enrollment and setup flows in a simulated environment.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with the OSS (Online Services Simulator) emulator used for testing printer setup and enrollment flows. Contains methods for navigating to the emulator, signing in, filling printer configuration details, selecting app types, starting emulator flows, handling OOBE and POOBE scenarios, accepting agreements, declining HP Plus, continuing through various setup screens, selecting country, and verifying trial-related UI elements.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OssEmulatorHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) directly.

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the OSS emulator URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() to navigate to the URL stored in common._oss_url. Waits for the page load state to complete using page.wait_for_load_state('load') with a 60-second timeout. This is the entry point for accessing the OSS emulator interface.

Signs into the OSS emulator by clicking the dev tab and then the HPID login button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dev_tab element to navigate to the developer section using oss_emulator_page.dev_tab.click(). Then clicks the oss_hpid_login button to initiate the HPID authentication flow using oss_emulator_page.oss_hpid_login.click(). This method assumes the user is already on the OSS emulator page.

Clicks the 3-month trial button in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) to ensure the page is ready. Clicks the yeti_3m button with a 60-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.yeti_3m.click(timeout=60000). The 3-month button is likely related to selecting a trial period for the Instant Ink service.

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, and fingerprint attributes

functions.what_it_does: Fills the OSS emulator form with printer configuration details. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Fills the serial_number field with the printer's entity_id using oss_emulator_page.serial_number.fill(printer.entity_id). Fills the sku field with the printer's model_number using oss_emulator_page.sku.fill(printer.model_number). Fills the uuid field with the printer's device_uuid using oss_emulator_page.uuid.fill(printer.device_uuid). Fills the cloud_id field with the base64-decoded postcard value using oss_emulator_page.cloud_id.fill(base64.b64decode(printer.postcard).decode('utf-8')). Fills the first fingerprint field with the base64-decoded fingerprint value using oss_emulator_page.fingerprint.first.fill(base64.b64decode(printer.fingerprint).decode('utf-8')). Selects 'completed' option for the language_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.language_config.select_option('completed'). Selects 'completed' option for the country_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.country_config.select_option('completed'). This method configures all necessary printer details for emulator testing.

parameters.name: app_type
parameters.description: Application type to select. If None, uses common._simulator_platform or defaults to 'Gotham'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects the application type in the OSS emulator. Sets app_type to common._simulator_platform if app_type parameter is None, otherwise defaults to 'Gotham' if both are None. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_tab to navigate to the application configuration section using oss_emulator_page.app_tab.click(). Selects the specified app_type from the app_type dropdown using oss_emulator_page.app_type.select_option(app_type). This allows testing different application platforms like Gotham, HP Smart, etc.

Starts the OSS emulator flow by clicking the start flow button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the start_flow_button to initiate the emulator simulation using oss_emulator_page.start_flow_button.click(). This triggers the emulator to begin simulating the printer setup flow.

Performs the complete OSS emulator setup flow by calling multiple helper methods in sequence. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to authenticate. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the 3-month trial. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill in printer details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the application type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the emulator flow. This is a composite method that combines all setup steps into a single call for convenience.

Configures the OSS emulator for Post Out of Box Experience (POOBE) flow by selecting specific checkboxes. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_post_oobe_agreements_features element to navigate to the POOBE configuration section using oss_emulator_page.app_post_oobe_agreements_features.click(). Clicks the subscription_checkbox to enable subscription-related features using oss_emulator_page.subscription_checkbox.click(). Clicks the oobe_checkbox to enable OOBE-related features using oss_emulator_page.oobe_checkbox.click(). This configures the emulator to simulate a post-setup enrollment scenario.

Performs the complete OSS emulator setup flow for POOBE (Post Out of Box Experience) scenarios. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to authenticate. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the 3-month trial. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill in printer details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the application type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.setup_poobe_flow(page) to configure POOBE-specific settings. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the emulator flow. This is a composite method for POOBE testing that includes the additional POOBE configuration step.

Accepts the connected printing services agreement during the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load') with a 120-second timeout. Clicks the accept_all_button to accept all connected printing services terms using oss_emulator_page.accept_all_button.click(). This is typically used during the initial setup flow to accept service agreements.

Declines the HP Plus offer during the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the last instance of the do_not_activate_hp_plus button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.do_not_activate_hp_plus.last.click(timeout=120000). Then clicks the decline_hp_plus button to confirm the decline action using oss_emulator_page.decline_hp_plus.click(). This is used in test scenarios where HP Plus enrollment should be skipped.

Continues past the dynamic security notice screen in the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the ds_continue_button (dynamic security continue button) with a 200-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.ds_continue_button.click(timeout=200000). The long timeout suggests this screen may take time to appear or load. This is used to proceed past security-related informational screens.

Continues past the value proposition screen in the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the next_button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.next_button.click(timeout=120000). The value proposition screen typically presents benefits of the service to the user. This method is used to proceed past this informational screen.

Selects the replace printer option and continues in the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the second instance (index 1) of the replace_printer_radio_button using oss_emulator_page.replace_printer_radio_button.nth(1).click(). Then clicks the continue_button to proceed using oss_emulator_page.continue_button.click(). This is used in scenarios where an existing printer is being replaced with a new one.

Activates HP Plus during the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the Hp_Plus_Continue_button to proceed to the HP Plus activation screen using oss_emulator_page.Hp_Plus_Continue_button.click(). Then clicks the Activate_HP_Plus button to confirm activation using oss_emulator_page.Activate_HP_Plus.click(). This is used in test scenarios where HP Plus enrollment should be completed.

Selects the country in the OSS emulator based on the GlobalState country code. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Uses page.locator() with an ID selector constructed from GlobalState.country_code to find the country element. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Scrolls the country element into view if needed using select_country.scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Checks if the country element is visible with a 100-second timeout using select_country.is_visible(timeout=100000). If visible, logs an info message with the country code using framework_logger.info(), clicks the country element, and clicks the continue_selection button using oss_emulator_page.continue_selection.click(). Waits 10 seconds using time.sleep(10) after the selection. If any exception occurs, the except block passes silently without failing. This method handles optional country selection that may or may not appear depending on the flow.

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Expected number of trial months to be displayed in the reminder text

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Instant Ink trial reminder checkbox is visible, checked, and displays the correct trial duration text. Locates the reminder checkbox using the data-testid 'instant-ink-reminder'. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the checkbox is visible with a 500-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify the checkbox is checked with a 2-second timeout. Locates the reminder text element using a class selector starting with 'styles__CheckBoxText'. Uses expect() with have_text() and a regex pattern to verify the text matches 'Remind me 2 weeks before my {expected_months}-month trial of HP U00a0Instant Ink expires.' where {expected_months} is the provided parameter value. Locates the trial info disclaimer using data-testid 'general-monthly-fee-disclaimer'. Uses expect() to verify the disclaimer is visible. Uses expect() with have_text() to verify the disclaimer text is exactly 'After the trial, a monthly fee will be automatically charged unless canceled.' This method validates the complete trial reminder UI including checkbox state and text content.

Clicks the Learn More button in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the learn_more_button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.learn_more_button.click(timeout=120000). This is typically used to access additional information or help content during the emulator flow.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper class provides comprehensive support for OSS emulator testing with 16 static methods covering all aspects of emulator setup and flow navigation. The class includes both granular methods for individual actions (click_3m_button, accept_connected_printing_services) and composite methods that combine multiple steps (setup_oss_emulator, setup_oss_emulator_poobe_flow). The fill_oss_emulator method demonstrates base64 decoding of printer credentials which is important for security testing. The class supports both standard OOBE and POOBE flows with separate setup methods. The country_select method uses defensive programming with try-except to handle optional UI elements. The verify_reminder_checkbox_and_trial_text method shows advanced validation combining visibility, state, and text content checks with regex patterns. The class uses GlobalState for environment configuration and framework_logger for logging. Several methods use long timeouts (120-200 seconds) suggesting the emulator may have slow response times or complex loading sequences. The class depends heavily on the OssEmulatorPage page object for element locators.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for overview page operations including cartridge verification, printer status validation, plan suggestion banner verification, plan upgrade information validation, unsubscribed status card verification, and invoice download functionality. Provides methods for validating various states and actions on the overview/dashboard page.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for testing and validating the overview/dashboard page including cartridge installation verification, printer status grouping validation, plan suggestion banner verification, plan upgrade information validation, unsubscribed status card verification with actions like re-enrollment and invoice download. Contains methods for verifying UI elements, validating text content, handling new page contexts, and file operations.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OverviewHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge) directly.

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k', 'c,m,y', 'cmy')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that specific cartridge types are displayed as installed on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter by comma to get a list of cartridge types. Defines a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type codes to their corresponding selector strings from overview_page.get_selector(): 'k' maps to kCartridgeItem, 'c' to cCartridgeItem, 'm' to mCartridgeItem, 'y' to yCartridgeItem, and 'cmy' to cmyCartridgeItem. Iterates through each cartridge type in the list. Strips whitespace from the cartridge type. Checks if the cartridge type exists in the cartridge_locators dictionary, raising a RuntimeError with a descriptive message if an invalid type is provided. Uses page.wait_for_selector() with the corresponding locator and a 20-second timeout to wait for the cartridge element to appear. Asserts that the cartridge element is visible using is_visible() with a descriptive error message. This method validates that the expected cartridge types are shown as installed on the overview page.

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: First cartridge type or combination to try verifying

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Second cartridge type or combination to try if the first fails

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to verify cartridge installation with fallback to an alternative cartridge configuration. Wraps the verification in a try-except block. First attempts to call OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge) with the first cartridge parameter. If a RuntimeError is raised (indicating the first cartridge type was not found), catches the exception and calls OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2) with the second cartridge parameter as a fallback. This is useful for printers that may have different cartridge configurations (e.g., separate color cartridges vs. combined color cartridge).

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Name of the printer status group to check (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive', 'Needs Attention')

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Expected number of printers - either 'only one' or 'more than one'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the number of printers in a specific status group section on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Retrieves all printer status section elements using overview_page.printers_grouped_by_status.all(). Initializes printers_grouped to None. Iterates through each status section element. Checks if the group parameter text is contained in the element's inner_text(). If found, assigns the element to printers_grouped and breaks the loop. Asserts that printers_grouped is not None and is visible, with an error message indicating the section was not found. Asserts that the group text is contained in the section's inner_text(). Locates all printer selector options within the section using overview_page.printer_selector_option and gets all matching elements. If the number parameter is 'only one', asserts that the length of the printer array is exactly 1. If the number parameter is 'more than one', asserts that the length of the printer array is greater than 1. If the number parameter is neither, raises an Exception with message 'Invalid number of printers'. This method validates printer grouping and counting on the overview page.

parameters.name: banner_type
parameters.description: Type of plan suggestion banner to verify - either 'downgrading' or 'upgrading'. Defaults to 'downgrading'.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a plan suggestion banner is visible on the overview page and contains the appropriate text for the specified banner type. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets the plan_suggestion_banner selector using overview_page.get_selector('plan_suggestion_banner'). Creates a locator for the banner using page.locator(). Waits for the banner to be visible with a 30-second timeout using wait_for(state='visible'). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible. Retrieves the banner text content and converts it to lowercase. If banner_type is 'downgrading', defines a list of downgrade keywords: 'downgrading', 'lower plan', 'reduce', 'save money', 'smaller plan'. Asserts that at least one keyword is present in the banner text using Python's any() function with a descriptive error message showing the actual banner content. If banner_type is 'upgrading', defines a list of upgrade keywords: 'upgrading', 'higher plan', 'more pages', 'increase', 'larger plan'. Asserts that at least one keyword is present in the banner text. If banner_type is neither 'downgrading' nor 'upgrading', raises a ValueError with a descriptive message. Prints a success message with a checkmark. Returns the banner locator object. This method validates that the system is suggesting appropriate plan changes based on usage patterns.

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: Expected number of pages in the upgraded plan as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the overview page displays the correct upgraded plan page count. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the plan_information element is visible with a 120-second timeout. Retrieves the inner text of the plan_information element using inner_text(). Removes periods and commas from the text using replace() to normalize the format. Uses regex findall() with pattern '\d+' to extract all numeric values from the text. Assigns the second number (index 1) from the extracted numbers to plan_pages variable, which represents the page count in the plan information. Asserts that the extracted plan_pages value as a string equals the provided plan parameter as a string. This method validates that a plan upgrade has been reflected correctly on the overview page.

Verifies that the unsubscribed status card displays all expected action buttons on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button is visible with a 150-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link is visible with a 30-second timeout. This method validates that all three key actions are available to users who have unsubscribed from the service: re-enrolling the printer, shopping for ink, and downloading past invoices.

Clicks the HP Shop Ink button on the unsubscribed status card and verifies it opens the correct HP shop page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that will open when the button is clicked. Clicks the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button. Retrieves the new tab page object from the expect_page context. Uses expect() with regex to verify the new tab URL contains '/shop/pdp' with a 30-second timeout, confirming it navigated to the HP product detail page. Closes the new tab using new_tab.close(). This method validates that the shop ink link correctly navigates to the HP online store.

Clicks the Enroll Printer Again button on the unsubscribed status card and verifies the enrollment flow is initiated. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Clicks the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button using overview_page.unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button.click(). Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new enrollment page that opens. Retrieves the enrollment page object and brings it to front using enrollment_page.bring_to_front(). Wraps the automatic printer updates handling in a try-except block. Attempts to call EnrollmentHelper.accept_automatic_printer_updates(enrollment_page) and logs success if the page is found. If an exception occurs, logs that the automatic printer updates page was not found or already passed. Creates a ConfirmationPage instance with the enrollment page. Uses expect() to verify the v3_content_area element is visible on the confirmation page with a 120-second timeout, confirming the enrollment flow reached the confirmation step. Closes the enrollment page using enrollment_page.close(). This method validates the complete re-enrollment flow from the unsubscribed state.

parameters.name: invoices_directory
parameters.description: Directory path where the invoice PDF should be saved. Defaults to '/tmp/downloads'.

functions.what_it_does: Downloads past invoices from the unsubscribed status card and verifies the PDF file is created. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link is visible with a 30-second timeout. Creates the invoices directory if it does not exist using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new PDF page that opens when clicking the download link. Clicks the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link. Retrieves the PDF page object and brings it to front using pdf_page.bring_to_front(). Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) to ensure the PDF is fully loaded. Constructs the PDF file path by joining the invoices_directory with 'invoice.pdf' filename using os.path.join(). Saves the PDF using pdf_page.pdf(path=pdf_file). Closes the PDF page using pdf_page.close(). Waits 2 seconds using time.sleep(2). Asserts that the PDF file exists at the expected path using os.path.exists() with a descriptive error message. Takes a screenshot of the main page showing the PDF file was downloaded and saves it to the invoices directory. Logs a success message with the PDF file path using framework_logger.info(). This method validates the invoice download functionality and file creation.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper class provides 8 static methods focused on overview page validation and actions. The class demonstrates good separation of concerns with methods for different aspects of the overview page: cartridge verification, printer grouping, plan suggestions, and unsubscribed user actions. The verify_cartridge_installed_message method uses a dictionary mapping approach for flexible cartridge type validation. The verify_multiple_cartridge_types_installed_message method implements a fallback pattern for handling different cartridge configurations. The number_of_printers_in_section method shows complex DOM traversal and counting logic. The verify_plan_suggestion_banner method uses keyword matching with multiple possible phrases to handle variations in banner text. The click_enroll_printer_again_and_verify_enrollment method demonstrates handling of new page contexts and optional intermediate steps with try-except. The download_past_invoices_on_status_card method shows file system operations including directory creation, PDF download, and file existence verification. The class uses Playwright's expect() for most assertions and includes appropriate timeouts. The class depends on OverviewPage, ConfirmationPage, and EnrollmentHelper for its operations.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for print history page operations including billing cycle selection, billing table data validation, currency value normalization and validation, FAQ validation with hyperlink testing, invoice download, notification verification, and plan information validation. Provides extensive methods for validating billing data accuracy across multiple billing cycles.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for comprehensive testing and validation of the print history page including billing cycle selection, billing data validation with currency normalization, FAQ section validation with hyperlink testing, invoice download functionality, notification verification, and plan information validation. Contains sophisticated methods for handling currency values across different locales, validating complex billing data structures, and testing interactive FAQ elements with nested links.
classes.how_to_instantiate: PrintHistoryHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call PrintHistoryHelper.verify_previous_billing_cycle_period(page) directly.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open the billing cycle selector. Clicks the select_1_option to select the first previous billing cycle. Uses Playwright's expect() with not_to_contain_text() to verify that the billing_cycle_period_title does not contain the text 'Current', confirming that a previous period was selected rather than the current period.

parameters.name: period_index
parameters.description: Index of the billing period to select (1, 2, or 3)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period from the dropdown and waits for the table to update. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it and logs the action. Uses conditional logic to select the appropriate period option: if period_index is 1, clicks select_1_option; if period_index is 2, clicks select_2_option; if period_index is 3, clicks select_3_option; otherwise raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating valid values are 1, 2, or 3. Logs the selected period index. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table_button is visible with a 10-second timeout, confirming the billing table has updated after the period selection. Logs that the billing table was updated successfully.

parameters.name: total_pages_printed
parameters.description: Expected total number of pages printed to validate

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the total pages printed value displayed in the billing table matches the expected value. Logs the start of billing table data validation. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Retrieves the inner text of the total_pages_printed element. Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numeric values from the text. Checks if the numbers array is empty and raises a ValueError with a descriptive message showing the actual text if no numbers were found. Asserts that the first extracted number equals the expected total_pages_printed value with a descriptive error message showing both expected and actual values along with the original text.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Either a locator object or a string selector

functions.docstring: Helper method to get text from a page element
functions.what_it_does: Retrieves text content from a page element handling both locator objects and string selectors. Checks if the selector parameter has an 'inner_text' attribute using hasattr(). If it is already a locator object, calls inner_text() directly on it and returns the result. If it is a string selector, uses page.locator() to create a locator, gets the first matching element using first, calls inner_text() on it, and returns the result. This utility method provides a flexible way to extract text regardless of whether the caller passes a locator or a selector string.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Currency value to normalize - can be string, number, or None

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values to a consistent format for comparison by removing currency symbols and formatting characters. Returns '0.00' if the value is None or empty. Converts the value to a string, strips whitespace, replaces commas with periods for decimal normalization, and removes common currency symbols (dollar sign, euro sign, pound sign). Removes non-breaking spaces (\xa0) and regular spaces. Checks if the normalized value is one of several empty or placeholder representations: '-', '', '\u2014' (em dash), 'N/A', 'n/a', and returns '0.00' if so. Wraps the float conversion in a try-except block. Attempts to convert the normalized string to a float. If successful, formats it as a string with exactly 2 decimal places using f-string formatting and returns it. If the conversion fails, returns the normalized string as-is. This method handles various currency formats and edge cases to enable reliable currency value comparisons across different locales.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Locator object or string selector for the currency field

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected currency value - can be int (cents), float, or string

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, checks if expected value is contained in actual text. If False, requires exact match. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a currency field displays the expected value with support for both exact and partial matching. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.get_text_from_selector() to retrieve the actual text from the element. Checks if the expected value is an integer using isinstance(). If it is an integer, divides it by 100 to convert from cents to dollars (e.g., 1299 becomes 12.99). Calls PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value() on the expected value to get the normalized expected string. If partial_match is True, normalizes the actual text by replacing commas with periods and asserts that the expected normalized value is contained in the actual text with a descriptive error message. If partial_match is False, calls normalize_currency_value() on the actual text and asserts that the normalized actual value exactly equals the normalized expected value with a descriptive error message showing both normalized and raw values. This method handles various currency formats and provides flexible validation for both exact amounts and amounts embedded in longer text strings.

parameters.name: expected_values
parameters.description: Dictionary containing expected billing values with keys like total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, total

functions.what_it_does: Validates all billing section fields against expected values from a dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each field in the expected_values dictionary, performs specific validation. If 'total_pages_printed' key exists, extracts numbers from the total_pages_printed element text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(), gets the first number, and asserts it equals the expected value. If 'pages' key exists, extracts numbers from the plan_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it equals the expected value. If 'rollover' key exists and its value is not '-', extracts numbers from the rollover_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it equals the expected value. If 'additional' key exists and its value is not '-', extracts numbers from the additional_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it equals the expected value. If 'plan_price' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with plan_price_text selector, expected value, and partial_match=True. If 'overage_price' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with overage_description_text selector, expected value, and partial_match=True. If 'previous' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with previous_billing_text selector and expected value. If 'charges' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with current_billing_text selector and expected value. If 'tax' key exists and its value is not '-', wraps in try-except to call validate_currency_field() with tax-amount data-testid selector, catching exceptions and logging a warning if the tax element is not found. If 'total' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with current_total_text selector and expected value. This comprehensive method validates all aspects of a billing cycle's data in a single call.

parameters.name: expected_data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries, each containing a 'billing_cycle' key and expected billing values for that cycle

functions.what_it_does: Validates billing data for multiple billing cycles by iterating through a list of expected data dictionaries. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Iterates through each row in the expected_data list. Extracts the billing_cycle value from the row and converts it to a 0-based index by subtracting 1 and converting to int. Logs the billing cycle being validated using framework_logger.info(). Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it and logs the action. Locates all billing cycle options using page.locator() with the billing_cycle_options selector from print_history_page.elements. Selects the specific billing cycle by calling nth() with the billing_cycle_index and clicking it. Logs the selected option number. Waits 1 second using page.wait_for_timeout(1000) to allow the UI to update. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section() passing the page and the current row dictionary to validate all fields for that billing cycle. This method enables comprehensive validation of historical billing data across multiple periods in a single test.

Validates all FAQ questions and their associated links on the print history page by expanding each question, verifying answer visibility, clicking links, and verifying navigation. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. Clicks faq_question_1 and verifies faq_answer_1 is visible. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new tab when clicking faq_terms_of_service_link, verifies the popup URL contains '/terms' with regex, and closes the popup. Clicks faq_question_2 and verifies faq_answer_2 is visible. Clicks faq_overview_link and verifies the overview page status_card_title is visible. Navigates back to print history using side_menu.print_history_menu_link.click(). Clicks faq_question_2 again and verifies faq_answer_2 is visible. Clicks the first update_plan_link and verifies the update plan page page_title is visible. Navigates back to print history. Clicks faq_question_3 and verifies faq_answer_3 is visible. Clicks faq_question_4 and verifies faq_answer_4 is visible. Clicks the last update_plan_link and verifies the update plan page page_title is visible. Navigates back to print history. This comprehensive method validates all FAQ interactions including question expansion, answer visibility, internal navigation links, and external links opening in new tabs.

Validates the print history page state when no pens (print events) exist by verifying certain elements are visible and others are not. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify print_history_section is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify page_title is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify print_history_card_title is not visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify how_is_calculated_link is not visible. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify total_printed_pages is not visible. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify plan_pages_bar is not visible. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify plan_details_card is not visible. This method validates that the page shows the basic structure but hides detailed billing information when there are no print events to display.

Clicks the Download All Invoices button with retry logic and verifies the invoices page opens. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Implements a retry loop with 60 attempts. In each attempt, wraps operations in a try-except block. Clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify download_all_invoices_button is visible with a 5-second timeout. If successful, breaks out of the retry loop. If an exception occurs and it is not the last attempt (59), logs an info message with the attempt number, waits 60 seconds using time.sleep(60), reloads the page using page.reload(), and verifies print_history_card_title is visible with a 90-second timeout before retrying. If all 60 attempts fail, raises an Exception with message 'Download All Invoices button is not visible after maximum retries'. After the button is visible, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens. Clicks the download_all_invoices_button. Retrieves the new tab page object. Asserts that the string 'invoices' is present in the new tab's URL with a descriptive error message. This method implements robust retry logic to handle cases where invoice generation may be delayed.

parameters.name: notifications_text
parameters.description: Expected notification text to find in the print history table

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text appears in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden using is_hidden(). If the table is hidden, clicks the print_history_table_button to expand it. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table is visible with a 30-second timeout. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) to ensure the table content is fully loaded. Retrieves the inner text of the print_history_table. Asserts that the notifications_text parameter is contained in the table content using Python's 'in' operator with a descriptive error message showing both the expected text and the actual table content. This method validates that specific notifications or messages appear in the billing history table.

functions.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price value as a string

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan page count as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information displays the correct price and page count on the print history page. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Retrieves the text content of the plan_price_text element. Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numeric values from the plan info text. Asserts that the first extracted number equals the plan_value parameter with a descriptive error message. Asserts that the second extracted number equals the plan_pages parameter with a descriptive error message. This method validates that the plan pricing and page allowance are correctly displayed.

parameters.name: total_value
parameters.description: Expected total billing amount as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total billing amount is correctly displayed on the print history page. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Retrieves the text content of the first current_total_text element using first.text_content(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numeric values from the total text. Asserts that the first extracted number equals the total_value parameter with a descriptive error message. This method validates that the total billing amount for the current period is correct.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the most complex helper class in the batch with 12 static methods providing comprehensive print history page testing capabilities. The class demonstrates sophisticated currency handling with normalize_currency_value and validate_currency_field methods that handle multiple currency formats, symbols, and locales. The verify_correct_billing_section method is particularly comprehensive, validating up to 10 different billing fields in a single call with conditional logic for optional fields. The verify_all_billing_cycles_data method enables data-driven testing by accepting a list of expected values for multiple billing periods. The click_download_all_invoices method implements robust retry logic with 60 attempts and 60-second intervals to handle delayed invoice generation. The validate_faq_on_print_and_payment_history method demonstrates complex navigation testing with multiple page transitions and popup handling. The class uses a mix of Playwright's expect() assertions and Python assert statements. The get_text_from_selector utility method provides flexible text extraction. The class depends on PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage for element locators and navigation. The currency normalization logic handles edge cases like em dashes, N/A values, and various currency symbols, making it suitable for international testing.

key_reusable_methods: HPIDHelper.complete_sign_in — Complete HPID sign-in flow with country dismissal and credential entry
HPIDHelper.complete_sign_up — Complete HPID sign-up flow with account creation and email verification
HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code — Verify account with email verification code using retry mechanism
